name: Sesame Crusted Halibut
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Halibut, cut into 4 equal pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Season fillets with salt to taste.
2. Brush the meat side of each fillet (not the skin side) with egg white.
3. Sprinkle sesame seeds on a plate.
4. Gently press each fillet's meat side down into the sesame seeds to coat evenly.
5. Melt butter in a large nonstick skillet over low heat, being careful not to brown the butter. Place the fillets, sesame side down, in the pan.
6. Cook until slightly golden on the bottom, about 3 minutes.
7. Turn each fillet skin side down and cover. Cook over low heat until the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork, approximately 4 to 5 minutes (note: cooking time may vary depending on the thickness of the fish; avoid overcooking as it can make the fish dry).
8. Garnish with lemon wedge.
